name: Trouble Shooting for the Zojirushi Breadmaker
recipeInstructions:
Your Result PLEASE TRY/BECAUSE
A. Loaf rises then falls Use buttermilk or vitamin D milk, decrease water by 1/8 cup, increase salt by 1/8 tsp.
B. Loaf rises then bursts Decrease sugar by 1/2 tsp., increase flour by 2-3 TBS.
C. Loaf is short/heavy Decrease salt slightly; increase yeast and sugar by 1/2 tsp.
D. Loaf is too brown/dark Set function to Lighter, decrease sugar slightly.
E. Loaf rises too high, hits viewing window Decrease liquid to 1 1/8 cup.
F. Smoke appears Ingredients (flour or other) spilled out side the pan on or around heating element.
G. Sides cave inward & loaf is damp & soggy Loaf was left in machine too long after completion.
H. Odd shaped loaf Reduce liquid by 1/8 cup.
I. Top is mushroomed Reduce liquid by 1/8 cup.
J. Flour residue on top Increase liquid slightly.
K. Loaf Overcooked Decrease sugar slightly and/or use lighter crust setting.
L. Clumps when slicing Let cool after removing from pan to reduce steam.
M. Inconsistent Heights/Shapes Inconsistent measuring and/or humidity or room temperature.
N. Raisins and other added Shake raisins or other ingredients in a plastic bag with 1 TBS of flour before adding.
O. Bread did not rise at all. Yeast was not added. Increase yeast.
P. Pan is Scratched/ Machine noisy If scratching persists contact office. Adjust for High Altitudes: Decrease liquid by 1/8 cup, reduce yeast by 1/8 to 1/4 tsp., reduce sugar by 1/2 tsp.
